title: will / shall
examples: DIY stands for do it yourself. There is even a TV channel called HGTV, which stands for Home and Garden Television. On that channel, they have a ton of shows [music] of people doing home makeovers, flipping houses, and house hunting. Behind me are some tools commonly used when doing [music] DIY projects. I see a level, a power drill, a hammer, and nails. But you'll also probably need a measuring tape.

I built one all by myself. It was so heavy and hard. I'm surprised I didn't throw my back out. Building IKEA furniture is not for the week, but I did it. I'm so proud of myself. I notice a lot of the furniture here has a very minimalist feel to it. And although I'm trying to live like a minimalist these days, my favorite style of home is actually the opposite, maximalist. In maximalist rooms, you'll find a lot of little trinkets, bold colors, fun patterns, and mixing and matching different styles. It sounds a little crazy, but if it's done right, it's really fun and whimsical.

That's a wrap [music] on my trip to IKEA. I didn't buy much, but I got [music] a lot of inspiration for my future home. Thanks for joining me, and I'll see you next time. Bye.

title: can / could / be able to
examples: I move around a lot, so I never really get to personalize my space or make it feel like me. One day I'd love to buy a fixer upper and do some renovations and remodeling like tearing out walls, redoing the kitchen, laying new flooring, and adding a fresh coat of paint. A nice lamp like this can totally change the vibe of a room.
It's like looking into the future. I can see myself here.
Honey, you're home. Our chef couldn't make it today, [music] so I was going to whip up something quick and easy like filet minan and lobster. What do you think?
I'm Dulu right now. Just let me be Dulu in peace. [music] Okay. A girl can dream. I think I found the cameraman's favorite room.

All right, vlog's over. Good night, everyone. [laughter] I could use a nap. It's quite comfortable. This mattress is a little bit firm, but I like it. Okay, let's keep going. This one is really cool because instead of having another bed on the bottom, they have a little love seat.

title: have to / must
examples: A love seat is a twoperson sofa. It's a cute word, isn't it? I think they call it a love seat since it's small. You have to get really close to the person next to you. Come on, cameraman. Sit next to me.
